title: astomaea galiocarpa
intro: Astomaea galiocarpa, also known as the "tree daisy", is a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Astomaea galiocarpa include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Astomaea galiocarpa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried and processed into capsules or tablets for convenient consumption.
